owning_department: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: Provide eligible entities with official DPIIT startup recognition so they can access Startup India benefits and related government facilitation.
eligibility: Entity incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company, registered partnership firm, limited liability partnership, multi-state cooperative society, or cooperative society; within 10 years of incorporation or registration, or 20 years for a recognised Deep Tech Startup; turnover in any financial year since incorporation or registration not exceeding INR 200 crore, or INR 300 crore for a Deep Tech Startup; working towards innovation, development or improvement of products, processes or services, or operating a scalable business model with high employment-generation or wealth-creation potential; and not formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business.
beneficiary_type: Eligible entities incorporated or registered in India seeking official DPIIT startup recognition.
benefits: DPIIT recognition provides access to Startup India benefits and facilitation, including easier compliance, intellectual-property support and eligibility to apply separately for applicable tax benefits and other government support.
application_process: Create an account on the National Single Window System; sign in to the NSWS dashboard; choose Add Approvals and then Central Approvals; find and add Registration as a Startup; complete the application and submit it through NSWS.
review_notes: Identity, ownership, eligibility, benefits, current service availability and NSWS application process are supported by official evidence. Exact current required-document checklist, formal user-guide URL and service-specific contact details remain under manual review. Funding fields and startup stage are not applicable to this recognition service.

canonical_name: Section 80-IAC Tax Exemption Eligibility Certification for Startups
owning_department: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: Enable eligible DPIIT-recognised startups to obtain certification for claiming the deduction available under Section 80-IAC of the Income-tax Act, 1961.
eligibility: Applicant must be a DPIIT-recognised startup constituted as a private limited company or limited liability partnership, incorporated on or after 1 April 2016, with turnover not exceeding INR 100 crore in any financial year since incorporation, and must satisfy the conditions under Section 80-IAC. The business must not have been formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business or by transfer of previously used plant or machinery, except where statutory exceptions apply.
beneficiary_type: DPIIT-recognised private limited companies and limited liability partnerships that satisfy Section 80-IAC eligibility conditions.
benefits: Eligible startups receiving certification may claim a deduction of 100 percent of profits and gains derived from the eligible business for three consecutive assessment years selected from the first ten years since incorporation, subject to the Income-tax Act.
application_process: Obtain DPIIT Startup Recognition first; open the official Form 80-IAC application on the Startup India portal; complete the application and declarations; upload the applicable supporting documents; submit the application to the Inter-Ministerial Board through the DPIIT portal; and monitor the application status online.
required_documents: Applicable documents include incorporation or registration evidence, shareholding details, board resolution where applicable, income-tax return acknowledgements, audited financial statements for the applicable years, chartered-accountant certifications and declarations concerning formation and financial information, and supporting material such as the pitch deck and application video as required by the official form.
review_notes: Identity and REQUIRES_DPIIT_RECOGNITION relationship are locked in v3.4.1.0.3. Eligibility, benefit, application route and principal document categories are supported by the official Form 80-IAC page. G.S.R. 127(E) is recorded as the service-specific notification evidence. Funding and startup-stage fields are not applicable. A consolidated guideline URL, service-specific contact details and the distinction between mandatory and conditional documents remain under manual review.

owning_department: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
objective: Increase access to venture capital across startup stages and sectors, with focused support for deep technology, early-growth ventures, innovative manufacturing and long-gestation technologies.
eligibility: Direct applicants are SEBI-registered Category I and Category II Alternative Investment Funds. Supported AIFs must invest in entities recognised as startups by DPIIT and comply with the applicable segment-wise conditions in the operational guidelines.
beneficiary_type: SEBI-registered Category I and Category II Alternative Investment Funds as direct recipients, and DPIIT-recognised startups as downstream investees.
startup_stage: Deep technology, early-growth, technology-driven innovative manufacturing, and sector- or stage-agnostic startups.
benefits: Indirect access to venture capital and mentoring through selected Alternative Investment Funds. The total scheme corpus is INR 10,000 crore, but startup-level investment varies by AIF and segment.
application_process: SEBI-registered AIFs submit detailed applications through SIDBI's Fund Application Portal and undergo eligibility screening, due diligence and consideration by the Venture Capital Investment Committee. Eligible startups may approach selected AIFs for investment; startups do not apply directly to DPIIT for FoF 2.0 capital.
review_notes: The official notification, operational guidelines and Startup India schemes playbook support the scheme identity, INR 10,000 crore corpus, SIDBI implementation, AIF eligibility, indirect startup funding model and AIF application route. The corpus is not recorded as a startup-level maximum. Exact AIF application documents, scheme-specific contact details and any additional domestic implementation agencies remain under manual review. VERSION_LINEAGE_FROM Fund of Funds for Startups 1.0 remains preserved without merging.
